videos.title: What is Forward Deployed Engineer (FDE) Role?
videos.description:
Hey everyone, in this video we are going to talk about a brand new software engineering role that is exploding in the AI era — the Forward Deployed Engineer (FDE). I share what an FDE actually does, why companies like OpenAI, Anthropic and our own startup Aksin (where I work as a Principal Engineer) are hiring for this role, and how it sits at the bridge between cutting-edge AI products and real-world business workflows. videos.title: Claude Can Now Dream - Memory of Agentic AI Explained!
videos.description:
Hey everyone, in this video we are going to talk about Claude's new "Dreaming" feature and the system design behind memory in AI agents. We start from the basics of LLMs being stateless, then walk through context windows, short-term memory (current chat thread) and long-term memory (extracted facts stored as key-value pairs and retrieved via RAG / vector search). Once the foundation is clear, we unpack what "Dreaming" actually does — Claude reflects asynchronously over past sessions, looks at the conversation as a whole instead of sentence-by-sentence, and reorganizes its memory store by merging duplicates and replacing stale entries. I also share a personal story from our own voice-agent product where we built a very similar concept called the "Subconscious Mind" before Claude Dreams was even announced.

videos.title: Nginx is Dead!
videos.description:
Hey everyone, in this video we are going to talk about whether Nginx is really dead and why modern microservice teams are moving toward Envoy proxy. We start with a quick recap of Nginx as a reverse proxy and load balancer, discuss the famous C10K problem it solved, and then dig into its biggest limitation — static, file-based configuration that needs a restart every time your upstreams change. Then we explore Envoy: a CNCF-backed, gRPC-driven proxy built for containerized, dynamic microservice architectures with the sidecar pattern, xDS service discovery, built-in rate limiting, circuit breakers and modern protocol support like HTTP/2, HTTP/3, QUIC, WebSockets, gRPC and even MongoDB. Finally we compare both tools head-to-head and see where each one still makes sense in real-world production.

Checkout My Courses:
- Master Docker Containerization: https://pro.piyushgarg.dev/learn/docker
- Master DSA with Java: https://piyushgarg.pro/udemy-java-dsa
- Master GenAI with Python: https://piyushgarg.pro/udemy-genai
- Master Backend with NodeJS: https://piyushgarg.pro/udemy-nodejs

Follow Me:
- Instagram: https://www.instagram.com/piyushgarg.official
- X (Formerly Twitter): https://x.com/piyushgarg_dev
- Personal Website: https://www.piyushgarg.dev
- LinkedIn: https://www.linkedin.com/in/piyushgarg195

00:00 Introduction & what this video covers

01:22 How reverse proxy and load balancing actually work

10:45 Modern protocol support (HTTP/2, HTTP/3, QUIC, gRPC, MongoDB)
11:14 Built-in rate limiting & circuit breaker

- Keyboard: https://amzn.to/4w228KQ
- Mouse: https://amzn.to/42Hxw3J

Video Titles

Nginx is DEAD? Why Modern Microservices are Moving to Envoy Proxy
Stop Using Nginx! Meet Envoy — The Future of Reverse Proxies
Nginx vs Envoy: The Reverse Proxy War Explained (with Sidecar Pattern)
Why Nginx FAILS at Microservices — And How Envoy Fixes It

Summary:
A deep dive into why Nginx — despite being the most widely deployed reverse proxy on the planet — struggles inside modern, dynamic microservice environments because of its static, file-based configuration model. The video walks through the C10K problem, Nginx's strengths in static content serving and SSL termination, and then introduces Envoy proxy as a CNCF-backed, gRPC-driven alternative built from the ground up for containers and sidecar deployments. We cover xDS service discovery, modern protocol support (HTTP/2, HTTP/3, QUIC, WebSockets, gRPC, MongoDB), built-in rate limiting and circuit breakers, and end with a balanced verdict on when to choose Nginx, Envoy, or Traefik in production.

videos.title: Vercel Hacked! Vercel April 2026 security incident #coding #backenddeveloper #programming
videos.description:
In April 2026, Vercel confirmed a security incident involving unauthorized access to internal systems.

But here’s the real story 👇

This wasn’t a “traditional hack.”
It was a modern AI supply chain attack.

👉 A compromised third-party AI tool (Context.ai)
👉 Connected via Google Workspace OAuth
👉 Used by a Vercel employee
👉 Became the entry point

From there, attackers pivoted into Vercel’s internal environment.

videos.title: I tried the tool that gives me and my AI agents infinite memory
videos.description:
Your OS forgets. Pieces adds a private, on‑device memory that captures your coding, docs & research, then answers, “what did I do today?” right inside your tools.

Checkout Pieces App: https://pieces.app/?utm_source=piyush&utm_medium=video&utm_campaign=y-piyush-001

Hi everyone, in this video, we are going to have a look at Pieces App — which acts as long-term memory for your operating system.
Pieces gives your OS the missing memory layer, allowing it to remember everything you do day-to-day across coding, research, chats, and tasks.

The best part?
Everything is stored locally on your machine, so your data stays private and secure — no cloud sync, no leaks, no worries.

In this video, you’ll see how I use Pieces to:
- Summarize my daily/weekly work for standups
- Track docs, PRs, and code snippets automatically
- Resurface past research (Next.js, TanStack, auth libs)
- Pipe memory into my IDE/LLM to avoid re‑explaining context

As a developer, I absolutely loved the concept. It has genuinely become one of my must-have productivity tools. I use Pieces to:
- Analyze my daily work
- Keep track of ideas and code snippets
- Manage stand-up updates and to-do lists
- And avoid losing context when switching tasks

Try it / Learn more

videos.title: Is JSON Dead? TOON is the new JSON
videos.description:
Hey everyone, In this video, we are going to see TOON which stands for Token-Oriented Object Notation (TOON) - JSON for LLM prompts at half the tokens. Official spec & TypeScript implementation.

Token-Oriented Object Notation is a compact, human-readable format designed for passing structured data to Large Language Models with significantly reduced token usage. It's intended for LLM input, not output.

videos.title: Master Role-Based Access Control Authorization with OpenFGA & Node.js
videos.description:
Hey everyone, In this video, we are going to have a look at Authorization and OpenFGA. We'll see how OpenFGA makes fine-grained access control more easy, scalable and flexible.

You'll learn how OpenFGA makes fine-grained access control easier, more scalable, and incredibly flexible for modern applications.

OpenFGA is inspired by Google’s Zanzibar paper — the same system that powers Google Docs, Drive, and Calendar. Whether you're building RBAC, ABAC, or something custom, OpenFGA has you covered.

Quick Links
Checkout OpenFGA: https://piyushgarg.pro/openfga
Code: https://github.com/piyushgarg-dev/openfga-demo

videos.title: Apache Kafka Crash Course | What is Kafka?
videos.description:
In this video, we delve into the fascinating world of Kafka, an open-source distributed event streaming platform. Join us as we explore the fundamental concepts and components of Kafka, including Kafka Topics, Message Partitions, and Consumer Groups.

Throughout the video, we will comprehensively understand Kafka's architecture and its significance in modern data-driven applications. Whether you're a beginner or already familiar with Kafka, there's something valuable for everyone in this discussion.

But that's not all! We believe in learning by doing, so we'll also walk you through a hands-on coding example using Node.js. By the end of this tutorial, you'll have a practical understanding of how to implement Kafka in your Node.js projects.

videos.title: Full Stack Twitter Clone
videos.description:
In this tutorial, we will create a FullStack Twitter Clone that allows users to create and post tweets, follow other users, and like, and view their own profiles and the profiles of other users.

► Checkout Course: https://learn.piyushgarg.dev/learn/twitter-clone?COUPON=EARLY_BIRD
► Coupon Code: EARLY_BIRD

► Checkout Detailed Document: https://www.notion.so/piyushgargdev/FullStack-Twitter-Clone-e9ec430db7674d7c92223054037848d7?pvs=4

Tech Stack
► Node.js for our backend code which would be running GraphQL server inside the node.js environment.
► GraphQL for our API, which provides a flexible and efficient way to define our data model and query it.
► Prisma ORM for our database ORM, which provides a type-safe and easy-to-use interface for interacting with our PostgreSQL database.
► PostgreSQL is our database, which is a powerful and reliable relational database system.
► Supabase for hosting and managing cloud Postgresql db.
► Redis for query caching on the server side and increasing query speeds
► Google OAuth for Sign in with Google
► JSON WEB TOKENS for authentication
► Next.js for our front, which is a popular framework for building React applications with server-side rendering and optimized performance.
► Tailwind CSS for styling and reusable components.
► Codegen for typesafe GraphQL queries and mutations.
► Graphql-Request as API client for client-server communication
► React-Query for client-side data caching and query caching
► Typescript to maintain code quality and write type-safe code.
► Amazon Web Services for storage and deployments.

Video Titles
FullStack Twitter Clone
FullStack MERN Application
Twitter Clone Using GraphQL

Tags
#nodejs #graphql #postgresql #reactjs #webdevelopment #programming #fullstack #mernstack #serverside #aws

videos.title: WebSocket in NodeJS | Socket.IO - Real Time Messaging
videos.description:
► Master NodeJS Playlist: https://www.youtube.com/playlist?list=PLinedj3B30sDby4Al-i13hQJGQoRQDfPo

Source Code: https://drive.google.com/drive/folders/1r7wEikJhd990t8Qdm7krNfOCY1DQKzf8?usp=sharing

In this video, we will explore the world of WebSocket and Socket.IO in Node.js. WebSocket is a protocol that enables two-way communication between client and server. It provides a fast and efficient way to exchange data between the client and the server in real-time.

Socket.IO is a library built on WebSocket and provides additional features like fallback options, multiple transport protocols, and rooms. It is popular for building real-time applications, such as chat applications, online gaming, and stock market tickers.

In this video, we will cover the following topics:
00:00 Introduction
00:20 What are WebSockets?
10:20 WebSocket Protocol Documentation
11:15  Socket.IO + Express
30:15 Testing Socket.IO Websockets
31:05 Homework

videos.title: WebRTC Tutorial - How does WebRTC work?
videos.description:
Take Advantage of WebRTC with ZEGOCLOUD SDK: https://bit.ly/438OzKM
Pre-built UIKits to build WebRTC apps faster: https://bit.ly/3OFu8ke
How to Build Flutter WebRTC Apps: https://bit.ly/437fOFv
How to Build WebRTC Live Streaming App: https://bit.ly/3OW8ys3

Hey everyone, My name is Piyush Garg, and in this video, we will understand the working of webRTC.

Now, what is webRTC? In simple terms, webRTC refers to web real-time communications. You can use webRTC to design video calling, audio calling, and chatting applications on the web.

Do you know what the best part about WebRTC is? The answer is, you don't need any backend to handle the communications. This means that two or more people are having video calls without any server, how? because WebRTC is implemented inside your browser. Isn't interesting? So, let's build our own video call application.

videos.title: Building Tic Tac Toe in ReactJS | React Tutorial Series | Complete React Tutorial Series in Hindi
videos.description:
Hey everyone,

I am Piyush Garg and I am going to start my youtube journey with react series. In this series, we are going to learn and understand react. Also after the completion of this series, you would be able to design and code complex react UI's and be ready for production-grade use of react.js.
